CustomerProfile.title: CustomerProfile
CustomerProfile.description: Represents a customer's profile, storing their personal and contact information. This entity does not store authentication credentials, which are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the customer.

lastName.description: The last name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer, used for communication.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the customer.

RestaurantTable.title: RestaurantTable
RestaurantTable.description: Represents a physical table within the restaurant that can be reserved by customers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RestaurantTable entity.

tableNumber.description: A unique identifier or name for the table (e.g., 'Table 1', 'Booth A').

capacity.description: The maximum number of guests that can be seated at this table.

description.description: An optional description of the table's location or features (e.g., 'Window seat', 'Quiet corner').

Reservation.title: Reservation
Reservation.description: Records a customer's booking for a specific table at a given date and time, including the mandatory booking fee.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reservation entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile who made the reservation. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N Reservation)

tableId.description: Reference to the RestaurantTable being reserved. (Relationship: RestaurantTable 1:N Reservation)

reservationDateTime.description: The specific date and time for which the table is reserved.

numberOfGuests.description: The number of guests expected for this reservation.

status.description: The current status of the reservation (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Cancelled', 'Completed', 'No-Show').

bookingFeeAmount.description: The mandatory booking fee charged for this reservation (default 50).

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity for the booking fee associated with this reservation. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 Reservation)

specialRequests.description: Any special requests or notes from the customer regarding the reservation.

MenuCategory.title: MenuCategory
MenuCategory.description: Groups menu items into logical categories (e.g., 'Appetizers', 'Main Courses', 'Drinks') for easier navigation and management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the menu category (e.g., 'Appetizers').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single food or drink item available on the restaurant's digital menu, including its details and image.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

menuCategoryId.description: Reference to the MenuCategory this item belongs to. (Relationship: MenuCategory 1:N MenuItem)

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, potentially generated by AI.

price.description: The current selling price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the menu item, displayed on the website.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently available for ordering.

allergens.description: A list of known allergens present in the menu item (e.g., 'Gluten', 'Dairy', 'Nuts').

FoodOrder.title: FoodOrder
FoodOrder.description: Represents a customer's food order, containing multiple menu items, and tracking its status from placement to delivery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FoodOrder entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N FoodOrder)

orderDateTime.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

deliveryAddress.description: The full delivery address for 'Delivery' orders, or pickup location for 'Pickup'.

deliveryInstructions.description: Any specific instructions for delivery (e.g., 'Leave at door').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and any fees/taxes.

status.description: The current status of the order, allowing owner to track delivery steps (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Preparing', 'Out for Delivery', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

estimatedDeliveryTime.description: The estimated time for delivery or pickup, for customer and owner tracking.

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity for the order amount. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 FoodOrder)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single item included in a FoodOrder, detailing the specific menu item and quantity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

foodOrderId.description: Reference to the FoodOrder this item belongs to. (Relationship: FoodOrder 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem ordered. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item ordered.

priceAtOrder.description: The price of the item at the time the order was placed, for historical accuracy and in case menu prices change.

notes.description: Any specific customization notes for this particular item (e.g., 'no pickles', 'extra spicy').

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records details of a monetary transaction, either for a table reservation fee or a food order, handled by an integrated payment gateway.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

transactionId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the payment gateway for this payment.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the payment.

paymentDateTime.description: The date and time when the payment was successfully processed.

paymentPurpose.description: Indicates the purpose of the payment (e.g., 'ReservationFee', 'FoodOrder').

reservationId.description: Reference to the Reservation this payment is for, if applicable. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 Reservation)

foodOrderId.description: Reference to the FoodOrder this payment is for, if applicable. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 FoodOrder)

definition.description: Stores a customer's personal and contact information. Only the customer themselves can access their profile. The `customerId` matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Represents physical tables in the restaurant. Publicly readable for reservation viewing, but only restaurant owners can create, update, or delete.

params.name: tableId
params.description: The unique ID of the restaurant table.

definition.description: Stores a customer's table reservation. Includes `customerId` for authorization independence, ensuring only the customer or an owner can access it.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer who made the reservation, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: reservationId
params.description: The unique ID of the reservation.

definition.description: Groups menu items into categories. Publicly readable for browsing, but only restaurant owners can manage categories.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the menu category.

definition.description: Represents a single food or drink item on the menu. Publicly readable, includes `imageUrl` and `isAvailable` status. Only restaurant owners can manage items.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the menu item.

definition.description: Represents a customer's food order. Includes `customerId` for authorization independence, ensuring only the customer or an owner can access it.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer who placed the order, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: foodOrderId
params.description: The unique ID of the food order.

definition.description: Details a specific item within a food order. Includes denormalized `customerId` and `foodOrderId` for authorization independence and efficient collection group queries.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer who placed the order.

params.name: foodOrderId
params.description: The unique ID of the food order this item belongs to.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

definition.description: Records details of a monetary transaction. Includes denormalized `customerId` (from the associated reservation or food order) for authorization independence, allowing customer-specific access and owner-level management.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique ID of the payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores UIDs of restaurant owners for role-based access control. Documents in this collection simply have their ID as the `ownerId`. Used for authorization checks (DBAC - Existence over Content).

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique ID of a restaurant owner, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for TableTap prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Mandate A):**
This is achieved primarily by ensuring that authorization context is present within each document, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and complicate debugging. 
*   For user-specific nested data like `Reservation` and `FoodOrder` documents, which reside under `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/reservations/{reservationId}` and `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/foodOrders/{foodOrderId}` respectively, the `customerId` field is explicitly included in the document. This allows security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.customerId` for ownership verification without traversing parent documents.
*   For `OrderItem` documents, which are nested deeper (`/customerProfiles/{customerId}/foodOrders/{foodOrderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`), the `customerId` (from its grandparent `FoodOrder`) and `foodOrderId` (from its parent `FoodOrder`) are denormalized into each `OrderItem` document. This enables robust, independent security rules and efficient collection group queries for owners.
*   The `Payment` entity, which can be linked to either a `Reservation` or a `FoodOrder`, is placed in a top-level collection (`/payments/{paymentId}`). To maintain authorization independence, the `customerId` associated with the payment (derived from the corresponding reservation or food order) is explicitly denormalized into each `Payment` document. This allows customers to view their own payments and owners to view all payments without complex `get()` calls.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B):**
Collections are designed so that all documents within them share a homogeneous security posture, simplifying rules and improving clarity. 
*   `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/...` paths are strictly private to the authenticated user and/or accessible by owners.
*   `/restaurantTables`, `/menuCategories`, and `/menuItems` are public-read collections, while write access is restricted to authenticated owners, defined by a separate role collection.
*   `/payments` is a top-level collection but restricted to the associated customer or owner. 
*   `/appRoles_owner` serves as a dedicated collection for storing owner UIDs, making role-based access control (DBAC) explicit and easy to check via `exists()`.

**Access Modeling (Mandate C):**
*   **Private Data & Hierarchical Paths:** Customer-owned data (`CustomerProfile`, `Reservation`, `FoodOrder`, `OrderItem`) follows hierarchical paths like `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/reservations/{reservationId}`, enforcing strong ownership based on `request.auth.uid` matching the path wildcard. This is extended to `OrderItem` for granular control.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The `/appRoles_owner/{ownerId}` collection implements DBAC using the 'Existence over Content' principle. An owner's role is determined by the existence of their UID as a document ID in this collection, enabling simple and performant rule checks (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/appRoles_owner/$(request.auth.uid))`).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports efficient and secure `list` operations (Queries Are Not Filters) by design:
*   **Customer-specific data:** By nesting customer data under `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/...` and including `customerId` in each document, customers can query their own data (e.g., `db.collection('customerProfiles').doc(uid).collection('reservations').get()`) with a simple rule `allow list: if request.auth.uid == customerId;`. The query path matches the rule, ensuring only owned data is returned.
*   **Owner visibility:** For the owner's dashboard, collection group queries (e.g., `db.collectionGroup('reservations').where('status', '==', 'Pending').get()`) can be used to retrieve data across all customers. The denormalized `customerId` (and `foodOrderId` for `OrderItem`) within each document, combined with checks against the `/appRoles_owner` collection, allows rules to efficiently distinguish between owner access and customer access without filtering, thus upholding QAPs. For public collections, `allow list: true;` provides efficient global access. 

This robust design ensures security rules are simple, atomic operations are protected, and both user-specific and aggregate queries perform optimally without risking data leakage or rule complexity.
